channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Dead fox now in hedge, 31st May
item.description:
Update - Dead fox was “in road”, now it’s been moved to lying in the hedge beside the pavement. In the hedge between the M&S car park exit and the exit/entrance to the back of Deli Nene. What three words tuned.salsa.counts. Thank you
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Church Avenue, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipped wooden pallet, 31st May
item.description:
Wooden pallet dumped on pavement leaning against lamppost near playground entrance.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Carter Place, Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti on cycle hangar, 31st May
item.description:
There is graffiti on the green cycle hangar. The word 'DERIK' is spray-painted in red, and there is white tagging next to it.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Chapter Road, Elephant and Castle, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipping, 31st May
item.description:
Two blue wooden pallets have been dumped against the brick wall on Penrose Grove, near the Gymnasium Kennington poster.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Penrose Grove, Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Section of motor vehicle dumped on the pavement, 31st May
item.description:
This item has been there for nearly a week

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 291, Croydon Road, Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Damaged alleyway affecting my fence panels, 31st May
item.description:
Hello, I’m writing to seek an update about a damaged alleyway which I reported a few months ago. I was advised the damage was passed on to the engineering department for review. However, I’ve not received any correspondence since to update me on the repair status. The alleyway damage is directly impacting my fence panels and concrete posts. This means that the temporary structure I have in place to secure my fence panels up are not 100% sturdy due to the alleyway damage. Please advise when the problem will be resolved. Thank uou

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Watling Street, Barnehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pavement blocked by overgrown front garden at 119 Shenley Road, 31st May
item.description:
The bush in the front garden of 119 Shenley Road is overgrown and has been allowed to grow to the point that it is blocking the pavement. It urgently needs cutting back to allow use of the pavement which is currently unusable.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 119, Shenley Road, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Someone has fly tipped some old white goods and other waste, 31st May
item.description:
Next to the footpath from Salter Road into the estate.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Salter Road, Rotherhithe, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.category: Fly-tip Small - Less than one bag

item.title: The branches and leaves are over hanging the road and pavement, 31st May
item.description:
Its really difficult and unsafe to walk along the pavement as the leaves and branches are causing a very small area to pass by. It is also very difficult for cars to drive along the road or see pedestrians. I feel unsafe as the trees cut out the street lights at night.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Border Crescent, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pot holes all over the road . This has been like this the last 3 months, 31st May
item.description:
Potholes everywhere. Under the bride at crossroads where people continuously crosding the road to get to train station. Will this ever be sorted out
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bromley Road, Shortlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Everyday fly tipping, 31st May
item.description:
Fly tipping residential/commercial rubbish next to a public bin. This is happening daily and has been reported many times already

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Nevill Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Extra roster to empty bins at half term, 31st May
item.description:
Half term is not a new thing. It’s hardly a surprise that there are many more park users this week. The park is full of litter, the bins overflowing, with more rubbish stacked on top of some of them. So can the roster be smarter in the summer holidays and next years May half term? There was 3 guys there fixing a railing (nice job), why couldn’t they have been emptying bins instead, and fixed the railing next week? Can the contractors work smarter and empty bins more frequently in high use? Can less used bins (eg the one at bind.gather.form) be moved to the playground? Litter attracts litter. This makes the park look even more neglected than usual.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Tetty Way, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
